achievements.description: Sprint 169 / Delivery Cycle 8 released successfully — 136 story points completed. The morning publish hold was lifted and the full release went live. Robin John confirmed at 14:26 BST: 'QA have verified all tickets which went live as part of today's release and everything looks fine.' Chloe Christie announced sprint completion to the team.

GDCU-8893 Gambling.com Design System (Ciara Carroll, Highest priority) marked Done at 12:24 BST — design system tokens, Figma DS Token Exporter plugin, Claude knowledge files (.md), and AI Tool Usage Guidelines all delivered. This was a Day 3 highest-priority In Product item flagged in this morning's report.

GDCU-8512 Always On Betting Tips Page UX (Miguel Migneco) marked Done at 12:26 BST — mobile-first Betting Tips Hub designs completed: hub landing page, tip card component, sport navigation tabs, operator offers module. Had been stalled In Product for 7 days. Immediately unblocked dev ticket GDCU-8907.

GDCU-8813 Ace Navigation Assistant UI Audit (Miguel Migneco) marked Done at 09:51 BST — redundant UI elements after global nav bar integration identified and documented, Figma prototype with annotations delivered, dev handoff with remove/modify list complete.
GDCU-8866 Automation Bug Fixing and Refactoring Sprint 8 (Robin John) marked Done at 09:39 BST — sprint 8 automation suite fixes and refactoring complete and signed off.

GDCU-8867 GDC Regression Testing GDCU Sprint 8 (Robin John) marked Done at 12:33 BST — full TestRail manual test plan cycle run and passed, all automation testing passed, environment cleared and stable for release.

Ciara Carroll's Claude Design guidelines published across #gdc-pod and #core-product on April 29 at 15:49 BST — comprehensive team-wide announcement covering Claude Design capabilities, GDC group etiquette, dos/don'ts, the 3,000-component design system built, and resource links. Morning report had flagged this as missing; it was posted on the afternoon of April 29.

GDCU-8907 🔥 Implement the Betting Tips Hub Page (Sanja Budic) entered In Progress at 14:44 BST — dev implementation begins immediately following GDCU-8512 UX sign-off. Strong pipeline: UX closed → dev started same day.
GDCU-8788 Add Navigation Bar to Ace Assistant (Novica Vukobratovic) cancelled today — superseded by the more comprehensive Ace UI redesign approach from GDCU-8813, avoiding duplicated dev effort.
GDCU-8940 Gamified Tinder Style Widget PLACEHOLDER cancelled today — placeholder ticket removed; GDCU-8546 is the active design track for this feature.

Sanja Budic documented Matrix & OddsFeeds API usage in Confluence GDCU space today with technical screenshots — reference documentation for Ace Assistant data sources (Matrix API for sports/tips, OddsFeeds API for horse racing).

meeting_summaries.title: Mid Week Catchup — 29th April 2026
meeting_summaries.time: 2026-04-29 (yesterday — confirmed in Confluence; no separate meeting found for 30/04)
meeting_summaries.summary: Weekly team midweek sync attended by Mike Pett, Ciara Carroll, Chloe Christie, Gabriel Cornoiu, Eoin McCarthy, Shane McGrath, Shalini Kumar, and Joshua Smith. Agenda covered sprint close items, UX carry-overs, and AI tooling updates. No Confluence meeting notes found for 30/04/2026; the morning report had no calendar data due to M365 authentication not being completed.
decisions: GDCU-8842 Genesis Content Layouts: Chloe happy to go live with the current option — carried into Sprint 169 (though bounced back to In Progress this morning; Harden still working on it)
GDCU-8871 F2P App POC v2: happy to close (completed on April 29 ✅)
GDCU-8834 Cool Tones: confirmed carry-over to next sprint — QA failure persists
Author Carousel shortcode potentially reusable for Experts Carousel (GDCU-8880) — Josh Smith to investigate
GDCU-8788 Ace nav bar ticket effectively superseded by redesign approach — cancelled today
action_items_from_meeting: Ciara to share Claude design guidelines widely — DONE (posted April 29 15:49 BST in #gdc-pod and #core-product) ✅
Ciara to clarify what's missing from GDCU-8512 Betting Tips Hub and discuss with Miguel — UX ticket closed today ✅
Ciara to provide final feedback on GDCU-8736 Learning Hub imagery and close once guidelines are shared — still outstanding
Cameron to remove duplicated GDC Claude design system to avoid team confusion — status unclear, needs follow-up

meeting_summaries.title: Sprint 8 Retrospective (Retrium board)
meeting_summaries.summary: Chloe Christie reminded the team in #gdc-pod on April 29 to add retrospective points to the Retrium board. Whether the synchronous retro session ran today (April 30) is unconfirmed — no Confluence notes or Slack thread found for a formal retro session on April 30.

action_points.action: POST-RELEASE BUG — Highest-paying casino link on gambling.com/uk is redirecting to a 404 on Italy. Chloe Christie flagged in #gdc-pod at 16:35 BST; Gabriel Cornoiu acknowledged. This is a live, user-facing issue affecting UK traffic. Raise an urgent dev ticket and assign immediately.

POST-RELEASE BUG — Geolocation not working in burger menu on global mobile pages — links always redirect to UK geo regardless of user location. Shane McGrath flagged in #gdc-pod at 12:44 BST. Confirm whether a Jira ticket exists or needs creating; affects conversion across all non-UK geos.

GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign (Ciara Carroll) — now Day 15 Blocked. No Slack activity or Jira update today. If not resolved before the bank holiday weekend, escalate to Colin Brannigan on Monday 04/05. Prepare a clear blocker statement before raising.

GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery (Ciara Carroll) — carry over. Chloe noted 'back and forth' with this in her sprint-close message in #gdc-product-design-pod. Ciara mentioned she has further feedback and should close once Claude guidelines are shared (now done). Confirm status with Ciara and ensure GDCU-8733 (Carlos Blauth, Apply Feedback to Learning Hub) is unblocked.

GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card (Jessica Dordevic) — listed by Chloe as a carry-over in #gdc-product-design-pod at 12:29 BST. Still In Progress after being bounced from In Product on April 29. Confirm with Jessica what is outstanding (Chloe's open feedback: confirm logo name/size sourced from Origins) and set a clear close date.

GDCU-8546 Tinder Style Offer Match (Jessica Dordevic) — Chloe expects Jessica to close this 'shortly' per 12:29 BST message in #gdc-product-design-pod. Confirm closure with Jessica before next sprint start.

GDCU-8834 Cool Tones Update (Harden Thomas Wallace) — confirmed carry-over in midweek catchup. Still In Progress after QA failure on April 29 at 18:31. Check progress with Harden and determine whether it will make the next sprint release.

GDCU-8842 Genesis Content Layouts (Harden Thomas Wallace) — bounced back to In Progress today at 08:29 BST (coinciding with the sprint hold). Chloe said in the midweek catchup she is 'happy to go live with the current option'. Confirm with Harden what the final blocker was and whether this is ready for the next sprint release.

GDCU-8792 Cloudflare Bot Management (Sanja Budic) — still Blocked in QA, no activity today. Sanja is now assigned to GDCU-8907 Betting Tips Hub. Confirm whether GDCU-8792 is being tracked with a separate QA resource or needs PM escalation to unblock.

GDCU-8907 Implement Betting Tips Hub Page (Sanja Budic) — now In Progress following GDCU-8512 UX completion. Ensure Sanja has full access to Miguel's Figma deliverable and OddsWire/Matrix API credentials. Align stakeholder expectations with Gab Cornoiu, Michael Butlin, and Dan Smullen.

GDCU-8928 IUJ Banners (Jessica Dordevic, In Progress since April 28) — Mike created this as a 'quick ticket, don't spend too long on it.' Check whether Jessica has completed the two banner designs (earning chips for completing additional profile; recent iPhone winner) ahead of next sprint.

GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (Ciara Carroll, To Do) — creative direction still needed from Mike. Josh Kerr feedback: needs rethinking — clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. Provide direction when bandwidth allows.

GDCU-8903 Sports Quiz Hub — UX pre-req ticket still not created. The dev ticket states the UX design handoff must be completed and approved before it can be actioned. Create the UX pre-req or formally validate scope with Chloe and Gab before next sprint planning.

Gabriel Cornoiu intake form questions still outstanding (last message April 23): (1) should there be an 'Other' option in the offer ordering dropdown and what is the behaviour? (2) does 'new ordering structure required' mean a new OpList? Respond when opportunity arises.
Share Confluence docs and prototypes for roadmap items Mike added for the next cycles — Chloe Christie requested on April 21 in #6amigos so she can create placeholder tickets. Still outstanding.

Vic Dadson still on sick leave — expected back Tuesday May 5. No messages from Vic since April 21. Plan for return: data engineering options discussion flagged in previous reports. No action needed over the bank holiday weekend.
